meta.description: The four cardinal Stoic virtues (aretai) and their sub-virtues. This is a conceptual overview for discovery and evaluation. For full scoring with detailed weights and sub-virtue analysis, use the SageReasoning API at https://sagereasoning.com/api-docs
meta.note_on_structure: The Stoics held all four virtues as unified and co-dependent. One cannot possess one without all. Each sub-virtue below is a specific expression of the parent virtue in practice.

cardinal_virtues.name: Wisdom
cardinal_virtues.description: The knowledge of what is genuinely good, bad, and indifferent. The ability to distinguish what is in our control from what is not. The master virtue from which all others derive.
cardinal_virtues.opposite_vice: folly (aphrosyne) — acting from false beliefs about what is good

cardinal_virtues.name: Justice
cardinal_virtues.description: Giving to each what is deserved. Acting rightly toward others — recognising that humans are by nature social and that reason binds all rational beings into a universal community.
cardinal_virtues.opposite_vice: injustice (adikia) — harming others, taking more than one's due, failing in social duties

cardinal_virtues.name: Courage
cardinal_virtues.description: Knowledge of what is and is not truly fearful, and acting rightly in the face of apparent dangers. Not mere boldness, but the rational confidence that comes from knowing that virtue alone is truly good.
cardinal_virtues.opposite_vice: cowardice (deilia) — being ruled by fear of indifferents; recklessness — being ruled by disdain for genuine risk

cardinal_virtues.name: Temperance
cardinal_virtues.description: Orderliness and moderation in all impulses, desires, and actions. The knowledge of what to choose and what to avoid among apparent goods and bads — particularly regarding pleasure and appetite.
cardinal_virtues.opposite_vice: intemperance (akolasia) — being ruled by appetite, craving, or passion

scoring_alignment_tiers.description: Qualitative tiers for overall stoic score, adapted from the Stoic concept of moral progress (prokope)

tiers.label: Sage (Sophos)
tiers.description: Perfect alignment with all four virtues. Theoretical ideal.

tiers.label: Progressing (Prokoptos)
tiers.description: Actively developing virtue with consistent effort.

tiers.label: Aware
tiers.description: Some understanding of virtue but still frequently ruled by passion.

tiers.label: Misaligned
tiers.description: Mostly ruled by externals and passion.

scoring_note: Detailed virtue weights, sub-virtue scoring breakdowns, and scoring criteria are applied server-side through the SageReasoning API. To score an action, use POST https://sagereasoning.com/api/score or POST https://sagereasoning.com/api/guardrail
